tossups.question: Do not read this question to Cornell A This artist won a third-place medal at the Paris Salon for a painting in which a crowd featuring various ages and races watches Mary, Martha, and Jesus as they raise Lazarus from his grave. This artist painted lions in several works, including a blue-shaded religious scene in which the lower half of the central figure’s body is cast in a rectangular beam of light. This artist of that version of Daniel in the Lions’ Den traveled in the Levant, inspiring works including one that he staged unconventionally as a peasant girl peering towards the beam of light appearing in her doorway; that work is this artist’s Annunciation. This man drew on his faith and race in a work depicting a poor African-American father and son praying before a meal. For 10 points, name this American artist of The Thankful Poor, best known for The Banjo Lesson.
tossups.question_sanitized: Do not read this question to Cornell A This artist won a third-place medal at the Paris Salon for a painting in which a crowd featuring various ages and races watches Mary, Martha, and Jesus as they raise Lazarus from his grave. This artist painted lions in several works, including a blue-shaded religious scene in which the lower half of the central figure's body is cast in a rectangular beam of light. This artist of that version of Daniel in the Lions' Den traveled in the Levant, inspiring works including one that he staged unconventionally as a peasant girl peering towards the beam of light appearing in her doorway; that work is this artist's Annunciation. This man drew on his faith and race in a work depicting a poor African-American father and son praying before a meal. For 10 points, name this American artist of The Thankful Poor, best known for The Banjo Lesson.

tossups.question: A figure’s upper half is obscured by shadows in this artist’s Daniel in the Lion’s Den, which is now considered lost. The White House contains a landscape created by this artist of Sand Dunes at Sunset, which features a hazy sailboat on the horizon. A small double portrait is visible in the corner of a room in this artist’s The Dancing Lesson. This artist depicted the angel Gabriel as a sliver of yellow light in his version of The Annunciation. (*) An old man and the child are depicted praying at the dining table in this artist’s The Thankful Poor; those same figures appear in this artist’s most famous work, which was possibly inspired by the literature of Paul Dunbar. This artist once spent a period of time in the Blue Ridge mountains, references from which show up in many of his paintings. For 10 points, name this artist of The Banjo Lesson.
tossups.question_sanitized: A figure's upper half is obscured by shadows in this artist's Daniel in the Lion's Den, which is now considered lost. The White House contains a landscape created by this artist of Sand Dunes at Sunset, which features a hazy sailboat on the horizon. A small double portrait is visible in the corner of a room in this artist's The Dancing Lesson. This artist depicted the angel Gabriel as a sliver of yellow light in his version of The Annunciation. (*) An old man and the child are depicted praying at the dining table in this artist's The Thankful Poor; those same figures appear in this artist's most famous work, which was possibly inspired by the literature of Paul Dunbar. This artist once spent a period of time in the Blue Ridge mountains, references from which show up in many of his paintings. For 10 points, name this artist of The Banjo Lesson.

tossups.question: This artist’s mother glows against a dark brown background in a portrait that echoes the poses of both Whistler’s mother and Thomas Eakins’ painting of Amelia van Buren. The story “Uncle Tim’s Compromise on Christmas” inspired the first painting by this artist to be accepted to the Paris Salon. In one painting by this artist, light from a single curtained window highlights two large, empty plates, a pitcher, and a bare wall as two people pray before dinner. After returning to Paris, this artist began focusing on religious scenes, such as their (+) Annunciation depicting Mary sitting on a rumpled bed with Gabriel on the left as a radiant beam of light. This artist’s The Thankful Poor shares similar themes with their most famous painting, where yellow firelight on the right and blue light from the window illuminate an elderly man teaching a young child how to play the title instrument. For 10 points, name this American painter of (*) The Banjo Lesson.
tossups.question_sanitized: This artist's mother glows against a dark brown background in a portrait that echoes the poses of both Whistler's mother and Thomas Eakins' painting of Amelia van Buren. The story "Uncle Tim's Compromise on Christmas" inspired the first painting by this artist to be accepted to the Paris Salon. In one painting by this artist, light from a single curtained window highlights two large, empty plates, a pitcher, and a bare wall as two people pray before dinner. After returning to Paris, this artist began focusing on religious scenes, such as their (+) Annunciation depicting Mary sitting on a rumpled bed with Gabriel on the left as a radiant beam of light. This artist's The Thankful Poor shares similar themes with their most famous painting, where yellow firelight on the right and blue light from the window illuminate an elderly man teaching a young child how to play the title instrument. For 10 points, name this American painter of (*) The Banjo Lesson.

tossups.question: This artist’s version of The Annunciation features a brown-haired adolescent Mary draped in Middle Eastern cloth and a golden blotch of radiant light in place of Gabriel. A faceless man in white robes holding a lantern provides the lighting to this man’s night scene, The Flight into Egypt. The businessman Rodman Wanamaker patronized this artist after he saw his Resurrection of Lazarus at the Salon of 1897; that act allowed him to make 1902’s The Seine and 1919’s The Arch. This artist was taught by Jean-Paul Laurens after he left the United States, where he studied under Thomas Eakins. This man depicted a father and his child praying at the table in The Thankful Poor. A young boy sits on the lap of his elder while receiving musical instruction in another painting by this man. For 10 points, name this Philadelphia born African-American artist of The Banjo Lesson.
tossups.question_sanitized: This artist's version of The Annunciation features a brown-haired adolescent Mary draped in Middle Eastern cloth and a golden blotch of radiant light in place of Gabriel. A faceless man in white robes holding a lantern provides the lighting to this man's night scene, The Flight into Egypt. The businessman Rodman Wanamaker patronized this artist after he saw his Resurrection of Lazarus at the Salon of 1897; that act allowed him to make 1902's The Seine and 1919's The Arch. This artist was taught by Jean-Paul Laurens after he left the United States, where he studied under Thomas Eakins. This man depicted a father and his child praying at the table in The Thankful Poor. A young boy sits on the lap of his elder while receiving musical instruction in another painting by this man. For 10 points, name this Philadelphia born African-American artist of The Banjo Lesson.

tossups.question: A Cincinnati bishop named Joseph Hartzell bought the entire exhibition of this man's paintings, which allowed him to move to France and win a medal from the Paris Salon for a version of Resurrection of Lazarus that innovatively emphasized Lazarus' response. He went to Palestine with J. Benjamin-Constant where he painted Jesus seated on a roof edge giving advice to Nicodemus. Mary sits on a bed with clasped hands and stares at a yellow light in his naturalistic Annunciation. In one of his best known paintings, a frying pan and a man's hat rest on the wooden floor as a grandfather instructs his barefooted grandson on how to play the title instrument. For 10 points, name this African American student of Thomas Eakins who painted The Banjo Lesson.
tossups.question_sanitized: A Cincinnati bishop named Joseph Hartzell bought the entire exhibition of this man's paintings, which allowed him to move to France and win a medal from the Paris Salon for a version of Resurrection of Lazarus that innovatively emphasized Lazarus' response. He went to Palestine with J. Benjamin-Constant where he painted Jesus seated on a roof edge giving advice to Nicodemus. Mary sits on a bed with clasped hands and stares at a yellow light in his naturalistic Annunciation. In one of his best known paintings, a frying pan and a man's hat rest on the wooden floor as a grandfather instructs his barefooted grandson on how to play the title instrument. For 10 points, name this African American student of Thomas Eakins who painted The Banjo Lesson.

bonuses.leadin: This painting’s composition resembles an illustration its artist made for Ruth McEnery Stuart’s story “Uncle Tim’s Compromise on Christmas.” For 10 points each:
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: This painting's composition resembles an illustration its artist made for Ruth McEnery Stuart's story "Uncle Tim's Compromise on Christmas." For 10 points each:
parts: Name this 1893 painting that may have been inspired by a trip to the Blue Ridge Mountains. This genre painting depicts a seated elderly man and a child holding the title instrument.
Henry Ossawa Tanner’s The Banjo Lesson was created for the Paris Salon, as was his 1898 depiction of this Biblical scene in which Mary gazes at a light beam representing Gabriel.
Tanner’s depiction of the Annunciation is now held in this city’s namesake Museum of Art. Tanner’s teacher painted an anatomy lesson held by Samuel D. Gross in this city.
parts_sanitized: Name this 1893 painting that may have been inspired by a trip to the Blue Ridge Mountains. This genre painting depicts a seated elderly man and a child holding the title instrument.
Henry Ossawa Tanner's The Banjo Lesson was created for the Paris Salon, as was his 1898 depiction of this Biblical scene in which Mary gazes at a light beam representing Gabriel.
Tanner's depiction of the Annunciation is now held in this city's namesake Museum of Art. Tanner's teacher painted an anatomy lesson held by Samuel D. Gross in this city.
answers: Philadelphia [or Philly; accept Philadelphia Museum of Art] (Tanner’s teacher was Thomas Eakins.)
answers_sanitized: The Banjo Lesson(by Henry Ossawa Tanner)
Philadelphia [or Philly; accept Philadelphia Museum of Art] (Tanner's teacher was Thomas Eakins.)

bonuses.leadin: This painting includes a partial self-portrait of the artist on the far right side, in which he is writing intently while looking over a railing towards the central scene. For 10 points each:
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: This painting includes a partial self-portrait of the artist on the far right side, in which he is writing intently while looking over a railing towards the central scene. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this painting whose foreground and background figures all wear black, in contrast to the white coats of a later painting of a similar subject by the same artist named for David Hayes Agnew.
This Philadelphia-based artist depicted himself studiously taking notes in The Gross Clinic.

In the Philadelphia Museum of Art, The Gross Clinic hangs next to a painting of The Annunciation modeled on a young Palestinian girl by this artist, a student of Eakins. This artist painted a prayer before a meal in The Thankful Poor.

bonuses.leadin: This work was painted opposite a similarly-titled mural by Mary Fairchild MacMonnies painted for the Chicago World’s Fair. For 10 points each:
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: This work was painted opposite a similarly-titled mural by Mary Fairchild MacMonnies painted for the Chicago World's Fair. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this missing mural whose central panel shows a figure wearing a red-and-white accordion skirt picking apples. This mural’s left panel depicts three figures chasing after a personification of fame.
Modern Woman was painted by this artist, who is more famous for her paintings like The Child’s Bath.

Cassatt’s drypoint of a woman in a blue dress holding one of these instruments was inspired by a figure on the right side of Modern Woman. An 1893 painting by a different artist depicts an old man teaching a young boy how to use one of these instruments.

answers: banjo [accept The Banjo Lesson] (The Banjo Lesson was painted by Henry Ossawa Tanner.)

bonuses.leadin: This artist created a portrait of Booker T. Washington in gray suit for the National Portrait Gallery. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this student of Thomas Eakins who depicted an old man and young boy praying at a table in The Thankful Poor. In another painting by this artist, a boy sits on what is believed to be his grandfather’s lap to receive a music lesson.

Another Tanner painting depicts an extremely illuminated version of this scene. Fra Angelico depicted the figure on the right side of this religious scene with her arms crossed in three depictions of it.

Tanner’s painting The Thankful Poor depicts a boy and his grandfather praying before a meal, similar to this painter’s scene of a family gathering around a lowered turkey as part of his Four Freedoms series for the Saturday Evening Post.
answers: Henry Ossawa Tanner (The second painting is The Banjo Lesson.)
The Annunciation [or Annunciation to the Blessed Virgin Mary; or Annunciation of Our Lady; or Annunciation of the Lord]
answers_sanitized: Henry Ossawa Tanner (The second painting is The Banjo Lesson.)
The Annunciation [or Annunciation to the Blessed Virgin Mary; or Annunciation of Our Lady; or Annunciation of the Lord]

bonuses.leadin: Works like The Arch and The Disciples See Christ Walking on the Water display this artist’s namesake palette of “blues.” For 10 points each:
parts: Name this artist who depicted a grandfather teaching a child on his lap in The Banjo Lesson.

This Philadelphia painter of The Gross Clinic taught Tanner. He depicted male nudes jumping into the water in The Swimming Hole, and painted a rower on the Schuylkill (“SKOO-kull”) in Max Schmitt in a Single Scull.

Tanner painted a version of this scene that unusually depicts one character as a beam of light. His contemporary George Hitchcock painted a version of this scene representing that character as a field of lilies.
answers: the Annunciation (Gabriel is the character unusually represented here.)

packet.name: C - MIT A, NC State, Waterloo A, Oklahoma A

bonuses.leadin: For 10 points each, name these 19th-century American painters.
parts: This man’s sea scenes include Fog Warning and The Gulf Stream, the latter of which shows a man in a tattered boat surrounded by sharks.

A child sits in an old man’s lap playing the title instrument in this artist’s The Banjo Lesson. This African American is also known for such religious scenes as The Raising of Lazarus and The Two Disciples at the Tomb.
This Hudson River School painter’s trip to South America was the inspiration for such works as The Falls of Tequendama and Cotopaxi.
